contracts.surface: project board catalog and state evidence v1
contracts.shape: One internal board.DiscoverCatalog result owns ref selection, plan topology, and per-slice state evidence for the #123 CLI and TUI surfaces. With no --release, sworn board --json emits {"releases":{"<release-name>":{"release":"<release-name>","sourceRef":"<fully-qualified-ref>","tracks":[...]}}} with release keys in bytewise lexical order. Every slice in tracks has the established fields plus stateSource and stateDurability. stateSource is a fully-qualified selected evidence ref or the literal working-tree; stateDurability is committed or uncommitted. The election considers only valid, topology-identity-matching status records from locally available eligible ref tips plus a dirty active working-tree record, uses the published lifecycle/attention/timestamp/durability/source order, and never silently treats a working-tree result as committed. When the supplied root has no usable Git HEAD, the same operation returns read-only filesystem catalog records in bytewise release order with empty sourceRef and matching file evidence projected as working-tree/uncommitted; it does not lazy-migrate or write files. Named sworn board --release <name> --json selects the same catalog result and retains top-level {release,tracks}; its slice objects include the same additive stateSource and stateDurability fields. sourceRef remains the topology authority, not a competing status rule.
contracts.rationale: A plan's source ref and a slice's farthest available lifecycle evidence answer different questions. Centralising both in one catalog prevents the CLI and TUI from independently rediscovering or ranking refs, while provenance makes an uncommitted winner operationally honest.
